sections.title: What 'Going to' Means
points: Use 'going to' for plans or intentions in the near future.
The structure is subject + be + going to + base verb.
Example: 'She is going to visit relatives.'

sections.title: Common Uses
points: We use it when the plan is already decided.
It is not the same as a sudden promise or offer.
Example: 'I am going to cook rice.' is a planned action.

sections.text: Note: For a quick decision or promise, use 'will' instead of 'going to'.

cards.fact: 'Going to' describes something you intend to do.
cards.mnemonic: Going to = a plan already in motion.
I am, you are, she is going to.

cards.fact: After 'going to' use the verb in its basic form.
cards.mnemonic: Going to play, going to eat, going to travel.
